# nomad_dynamic_host_volume (Resource)

Creates and registers a dynamic host volume in Nomad. Note that Nomad supports two workflows for dynamic host volumes: create and register. Both resources result in the same data source with the same outputs.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource nomad_dynamic_host_volume "example" {
  name      = "example"
  namespace = "prod"
  plugin_id = "mkdir"

  capacity_max = "12 GiB"
  capacity_min = "1.0 GiB"

  capability {
    access_mode     = "single-node-writer"
    attachment_mode = "file-system"
  }

  constraint {
    attribute = "$${attr.kernel.name}"
    value     = "linux"
  }

  parameters = {
    some_key = "some_value"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `capability` (Block List, Min: 1) Capability (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--capability))
- `name` (String) Volume name
- `plugin_id` (String) Plugin ID

### Optional

- `capacity_max` (String) Requested maximum capacity
- `capacity_min` (String) Requested minimum capacity
- `constraint` (Block List) Constraints (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--constraint))
- `namespace` (String) Volume namespace
- `node_id` (String) Node ID
- `node_pool` (String) Node pool
- `parameters` (Map of String) Parameters

### Read-Only

- `capacity` (String) Provisioned capacity
- `capacity_bytes` (Number)
- `capacity_max_bytes` (Number)
- `capacity_min_bytes` (Number)
- `host_path` (String) Host path
- `id` (String) Volume ID
- `state` (String) State

<a id="nestedblock--capability"></a>
### Nested Schema for `capability`

Required:

- `access_mode` (String) An access mode available for the volume.
- `attachment_mode` (String) An attachment mode available for the volume.


<a id="nestedblock--constraint"></a>
### Nested Schema for `constraint`

Required:

- `attribute` (String) An attribute to check to constrain volume placement

Optional:

- `operator` (String) The operator to use for comparison
- `value` (String) The requested value of the attribute
